sourceMovie.title: Captain Phillips
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Captain Phillips for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Greengrass keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blood Diamond
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Captain Phillips clicked for you, Blood Diamond (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Captain Phillips?
faq.answer: Try Blood Diamond, The Sum of All Fears, The Kingdom—each shares DNA with Captain Phillips while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Captain Phillips?
faq.answer: Captain Phillips (2013) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Captain Phillips worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
